title: OpenBindings Operation Graph Definition (v0.2.0)
description: JSON Schema for an openbindings.operation-graph definition — the addressable unit of this binding format. A binding's ref resolves (via JSON Pointer) to a value matching this schema. The shape of the enclosing JSON document is not constrained by this specification. Structural validation only; behavioral rules (identity law, back-closure, cycle rules) are defined by the specification prose, which governs. Working draft accompanying the v0.2.0 specification working draft; not yet tagged.

OperationGraph.description: An operation graph: a directed graph of typed nodes connected by edges, in which every processing node is an operation invocation — bound at runtime (operation) or bound by the specification (built-ins).

openbindings.operation-graph.description: Format version this graph was authored against (SemVer 2.0.0, e.g., 0.2.0). Each graph declares its own version independently.

description.description: A human-readable description of what the operation graph does.

nodes.description: Map of named nodes. Each key is a node identifier.

edges.description: Array of edges connecting nodes. Edges carry event streams. Duplicate edges (same from/to pair) are forbidden by OG-V-08; uniqueItems backstops that rule structurally.

Node.description: A typed node. Every node receives the merged streams of its incoming edges and emits a stream along its outgoing edges. The 'type' field is the discriminator: it determines the node's behavior and which other fields are valid.

type.description: The node type. 'operation' is the cardinality-agnostic conduit (one invocation per graph invocation); the remaining processing types are built-ins — operations whose binding is the specification. Must be one of the types defined by this format version (OG-V-14); custom node types require a distinct format token or profile.

onError.description: Node key to route error events to if this node fails. Processing nodes only; not permitted on the boundary nodes (input, output), which are not invocations of their own. If not set, per-event failures are dropped and an operation conduit's terminal error terminates the graph invocation.

operation.description: Operation key in the containing OBI to invoke. The node holds one invocation per graph invocation and pipes its incoming stream into it: each arriving event is written as one input, incoming completion closes the invocation's input side, and outputs flow downstream in emission order. Cardinality-agnostic; MUST NOT participate in a cycle (OG-V-10). An unhandled terminal error on the held invocation terminates the graph invocation with that error; onError opts it into in-graph handling instead.

timeout.description: Total budget in milliseconds for the held invocation, from graph-invocation startup until its output stream completes; not an idle or per-event timeout. On expiry the invocation terminates with TIMEOUT_EXCEEDED, handled as a conduit terminal error.

operation.description: Operation key in the containing OBI to invoke once per arriving event: one invocation per event, the event written as its only input, outputs merged downstream.

maxIterations.description: Maximum invocations this node may open per event lineage. Required if the node is part of a cycle (OG-V-09). Events whose lineage count has reached the limit are dropped, not errored.

timeout.description: Per-invocation budget in milliseconds, from drive until that invocation's output stream completes. On expiry, that invocation fails as a node failure for the triggering event.

limit.description: Flush after accumulating this many events, then reset and continue (a tumbling window of disjoint batches). Takes precedence over until/through when both would fire on the same event.

until.description: JSON Schema. Flush when an event matches (exclusive — matching event not included and dropped).

through.description: JSON Schema. Flush when an event matches (inclusive — matching event is included).

schema.description: JSON Schema. Events that validate pass; others are dropped.

transform.description: Transform expression. Events where the expression evaluates truthy pass; others are dropped.
Transform expression. The result replaces the event.
Transform expression. The result MUST be an array; each element is emitted as a separate event.

error.description: If true, the operation graph terminates with an error. If false (default), the event is emitted to output before termination.

Edge.description: A connection between two nodes, carrying an event stream. Edges carry no logic.

from.description: The key of the source node.

to.description: The key of the target node.
